value.message: Unauthorized: user is not allowed to perform this operation
An instance with the requested name already exists in the project.
The instance is not yet ready to perform this operation.
A required downstream service is currently unresponsive
сan not delete user while active databases exist.

roles.description: A list containing the user roles for the instance. Please refer to the List Roles endpoint for a list of all available Roles.

code.description: The http error code of the error.

message.description: More detailed information about the error.

traceId.description: The trace id of the request.

type.description: Describes in which state the api was when the error happened.

backups.description: The list containing the information about the backups.

collations.description: List of collations available for the instance.

databases.description: A list containing all databases for the instance.

ListFlavors.description: The flavor of the instance containing the technical features.

flavors.description: List of flavors available for the project.

instances.description: List of owned instances and their current state.

roles.description: List of all role names available in the instance

users.description: List of all users inside an instance

versions.description: A list containing available postgres versions.

roles.description: A list containing the user roles for the instance. Please refer to the List Roles endpoint for a list of all available Roles.
A list containing the user roles for the instance. Please refer to the List Roles endpoint for a list of all available Roles.

code.description: the http error should be always 422 for validationError

validation.description: errors for all fields where the error happened

backup.completionTime.description: The time when the backup was completed in RFC3339 format.

backup.recoveryTime.description: the time for the point in time recovery it will be calculated between first backup and last backup

backup.retainedUntil.description: The time until the backup will be retained.

backup.schedule.description: The schedule for when the database backup will be created. Currently, ONLY daily schedules are supported (every 24 hours). The schedule is written as a cron schedule.

backup.schedule.opt.description: The schedule for when the database backup will be created. Currently, ONLY daily schedules are supported (every 24 hours). The schedule is written as a cron schedule.

backup.size.description: The size of the backup in bytes.

backup.storage.class.description: The storage class for the storage.

backup.type.description: The type of the backup, which can be automated or manual triggered.

clone.name.opt.description: The name of the cloned instance. If not provided, the default naming behavior of appending '-clone' to the source instance name is used.

clone.pointInTime.description: the time for the point in time recovery it will be calculated between first backup and last backup

clone.storage.class.description: The storage class for the storage.

database.roles.description: The name and the roles for a database for a user.

roles.description: The name and the roles for a database

flavor.cpu.description: The cpu count of the instance.

flavor.flavorId.description: The id of the instance flavor.

flavor.flavorId.opt.description: The id of the instance flavor.

flavor.maxGB.description: maximum storage which can be ordered for the flavor in Gigabyte.

flavor.memory.description: The memory of the instance in Gibibyte.

flavor.minGB.description: minimum storage which is required to order in Gigabyte.

flavor.nodeType.description: defines the nodeType it can be either single or replica

flavor.storageClasses.description: maximum storage which can be ordered for the flavor in Gigabyte.

flavor.storageClasses.storageClass.description: a storageClass defines how efficient the storage can work

instance.connectionInfo.description: The connection information of the instance

instance.connectionInfo.write.description: The DNS name and port in the instance overview

instance.encryption.description:
The configuration for instance's volume and backup storage encryption.

⚠️ **Note:** This feature is in private preview. Supplying this object is only permitted for enabled accounts. If your account does not have access, the request will be rejected.

instance.isDeletable.description: Whether the instance can be deleted or not.

instance.network.description: The access configuration of the instance

instance.network.accessScope.description: The access scope of the instance. It defines if the instance is public or airgapped.

instance.network.create.description: The access configuration of the instance

instance.network.opt.description: The access configuration of the instance

instance.network.update.description: The access configuration of the instance

instance.storageRange.description: Contains the minimum and maximum storage space.

instance.version.description: The Postgres version used for the instance. See [Versions Endpoint](/documentation/postgres-flex-service/version/v3beta1#tag/Version) for supported version parameters.

instance.version.update.description: The Postgres version used for the instance. See [Versions Endpoint](/documentation/postgres-flex-service/version/v3beta1#tag/Version) for supported version parameters.

instance.version.update.opt.description: The Postgres version used for the instance. See [Versions Endpoint](/documentation/postgres-flex-service/version/v3beta1#tag/Version) for supported version parameters.

labels.description:
Key-value pairs, 63 characters max, begin and end with an alphanumerical character,
may contain dashes (-), underscores (_), dots (.), and alphanumerics between. Key MUST be at least 1 character.
Max 64 labels
Regex for keys: ^(?=.{1,63}$)([A-Za-z0-9][-A-Za-z0-9_.]*)?[A-Za-z0-9]$
Regex for values: ^(?=.{0,63}$)(([A-Za-z0-9][-A-Za-z0-9_.]*)?[A-Za-z0-9])*$
The stackit- prefix is reserved and cannot be used for Keys.

retentionDays.description: How long backups are retained. The value can only be between 32 and 90 days.

retentionDays.opt.description: How long backups are retained. The value can only be between 32 and 90 days.

state.description: The current state of the instance.

storage.description: The object containing information about the storage size and class.

storage.class.description: The storage class for the storage.

storage.class.create.description: The storage class for the storage.

storage.create.description: The object containing information about the storage size and class.

storage.max.description: The maximum available amount of storage.

storage.min.description: The minimum available amount of storage.

storage.update.description: The object containing information about the storage size and class.

timestamp.description: The timestamp should be specified RFC3339 format.

user.role.description:
The user role for the instance. Valid roles depend on the Postgres version of the instance;
retrieve the allowed roles via GET roles endpoint.

user.state.description: The current state of the user.

version.description: The version of the postgres instance and more details.

beta.description: Flag if the version is a beta version. If set the version may contain bugs and is not fully tested.

deprecated.description: Timestamp in RFC3339 format which says when the version will no longer be supported by STACKIT.

recommend.description: Flag if the version is recommend by the STACKIT Team.

version.description: The postgres version used for the instance.

info.description: ⚠️ This API is deprecated. It will be retired on 01.11.2027. Please use the STACKIT PostgreSQL Flex API v3 instead.
info.title: STACKIT PostgreSQL Flex API (deprecated)
